(b) a body line that IS a reply sentinel is neutralized on write (safeBody inserts a ZWSP â†’ '<!--\u200breply:'): 0 phantom replies, after-sentinel text survives (no truncation), a genuine reply still parses (countâ†’1). spex lint = 0 error throughout.

On the trunk with the pre-commit hook active: (1) 'spex issues open' lands a store commit because the writer commits --no-verify â€” bypasses the hook, needs no guard exception. (2) a plain non-store commit is BLOCKED ('direct commits on main are blocked'). (3) a plain store-only commit (no --no-verify) is blocked TOO â€” main-guard carries no .spec/.issues special-case, so it stays the single clean question 'am I committing directly onto the trunk?'. (4) SPEXCODE_ALLOW_MAIN=1 lets it through, proving the guard is the blocker.

Default (no config) = ON. 'spex issues off' writes issues.enabled:false to spexcode.json â†’ status OFF, the next --no-ff node merge prints NO post-merge nudge and 'spex issues nudge <node>' is empty. 'spex issues on' restores the nudge on the following merge (names the node). spexcode.json is the single source of truth and a pre-rename proposals.enabled:false still reads as OFF. The git post-merge hook honors the switch through the CLI.

Deterministic repro from the issue thread: resolving an already-landed thread to landed is a no-change store write; writeStoreFile's writeâ†’addâ†’commit chain hits 'nothing to commit' and git commit exits 1, so the CLI reports 'Command failed: git ... commit --no-verify ...' exit 1 â€” an idempotent success mistaken for a failure. Duplicate sign has the same bug.

Fixed: writeStoreFile detects the no-op after staging (staged == HEAD) and skips the commit; commitStore surfaces changed. Same repro command now prints 'already landed â€” store unchanged' exit 0 with trunk HEAD untouched; duplicate sign exits 0; genuine open/sign/resolve writes still land as exactly one commit each (throwaway-repo transcript).
